input:
Indeed, ʿUthmān was the first man ever to emigrate with his wife for no other reason than serving God’s cause since Prophet Lot had emigrated many centuries earlier. Those sixteen people managed to find a boat which carried them to their destination. Apparently, they were chased by the Quraysh, who sought to force them to return but their pursuers arrived at the coast when the boat had already set sail. Other parties of emigrants followed at frequent intervals. About ten of those who had emigrated to Abyssinia soon returned home, not because they found their new abode unwelcome, but because they heard that the Quraysh had accepted the message of Prophet Muhammad and embraced Islam.
Source: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 10: Moving Out

output.scene_title: Uthman's First Migration with His Wife
output.source_reference: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 10: Moving Out

characters.name: Uthman ibn Affan
characters.appearance: Early 30s, light olive skin, athletic build, slightly above average height. Trim beard with slight reddish henna tint, neatly kept. Wearing a simple white thobe, off-white woolen cloak, white cloth wrapped as loose head covering. Worn leather sandals. Confident, resolute eyes.

characters.name: Umm Amarah (Umm Amarah bint Jarwal)
characters.appearance: Early 30s, light olive skin, slender build, slightly shorter than average. Simple headscarf covering her hair, modestly dressed in a long white thobe with brown cloak draped over her shoulders. Kind gentle eyes, smiling quietly.

characters.name: Makkans
characters.appearance: Group of variously dressed men in 7th century Makkah attire, indistinct features, blurred in the background.

output.moral_lesson: Faith requires courage and resilience in the face of adversity.
output.narrative_text: Uthman, the first man to emigrate with his wife for the sake of God, bravely escaped the Quraysh's pursuit and found refuge in Abyssinia. Others soon followed, but some returned home when they heard the Quraysh had accepted Islam.

compliance.notes: No Prophet Muhammad shown in the scene. Modestly dressed women and crowd shown in silhouette. Scene focused on Uthman's bravery and faith.

output.image_prompt: Watercolor illustration of a 7th century Makkah coastal scene at sunset. Silhouetted figures of men in traditional attire, including a man and woman in white thobes and brown cloaks, fleeing from a group of Quraysh men in dark robes. A small boat in the distance, leaving the shore. Blurred, impressionistic background with warm golden light. No modern objects.
